response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced software craftsman with a strong background in TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web/mobile development, but the mandatory requirements for this role are unmet. C++ experience is minimal—limited to approximately six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) writing C/C++ components and a bachelor thesis side project—far short of the required 5–10 years. There is no evidence of Windows platform development, SQL Server or Oracle proficiency, or meaningful capital markets or fixed-income domain knowledge; a brief stint at RIDE Capital (a German fintech startup focused on retail investment) does not constitute capital markets or investment banking domain expertise.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering background with demonstrated ability to work across the stack and pick appropriate tools per context
Strong CI/CD, DevOps, and developer-tooling experience (GitLab, Docker, Kubernetes, trunk-based development) aligns with optional desirable skills
Genuine fintech-adjacent exposure at RIDE Capital, applying software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing) in a regulated financial product context
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months total across entire career) against a mandatory 5–10 year requirement on Windows platform
No SQL Server or Oracle experience identified; SQL is listed as a generic skill with MongoDB and MySQL as primary databases
Zero fixed-income, fixed-income derivatives, or capital markets domain knowledge; primary domain experience is web, mobile, and geospatial platforms
